name: AgenticMail MCP Server
what_it_does: AgenticMail MCP server providing AI agents with email capabilities — reading, searching, and sending emails via IMAP/SMTP, managing mailbox folders, handling email drafts, and integrating email communication into agent-driven workflow automation and personal assistant applications.
use_cases: Reading and searching emails from inbox management agents
Sending emails on behalf of users from communication agents
Organizing emails into folders from email triage agents
Monitoring inbox for specific emails from workflow trigger agents
Drafting and scheduling email replies from personal assistant agents
Building email-driven automation workflows from productivity agents
not_for: Teams needing calendar integration (use Google Calendar or Outlook MCPs)
Teams requiring enterprise email features (Exchange, Outlook-specific features)
best_when: An AI agent needs to read and send personal or business email via standard IMAP/SMTP protocols — particularly for personal assistant workflows and inbox management automation.
avoid_when: You need calendar features, enterprise email compliance, or high-volume transactional sending.

auth.notes: IMAP/SMTP credentials required. Supports app passwords (Gmail, Outlook) and standard IMAP credentials. OAuth2 may be supported for Gmail. Store credentials as environment variables.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP server. Email provider costs (Gmail, Outlook, etc.) depend on your email service plan.

performance.rate_limits: Email provider rate limits apply (Gmail: 500 sends/day free tier)

known_agent_gotchas: Gmail requires App Password (not regular password) when 2FA is enabled
Outlook/Microsoft requires OAuth2 or App Password — regular password blocked
Email send actions are irreversible — always confirm before sending
IMAP connection may require enabling IMAP access in email provider settings
Community MCP from individual contributor — limited maintenance guarantee
Attachment handling may be limited — verify support for your use case
agent_readiness.security_notes: Email credentials — use App Passwords not main account password. TLS for IMAP/SMTP. Store credentials in env var. Community MCP. Email sends are irreversible — confirm before executing.

security_score_components.security_notes: App Password auth. TLS enforced for IMAP/SMTP. Store credentials in env var. Community MCP. Irreversible sends — confirm before executing.
